reviews.review_description:
First and foremost, this is a card battler TTRPG set in the Faerun and using D&D abilities for the actions that your party (of four) can take. It does not strictly use D&D mechanics, however. 

But that's fine. This is a rare gem of a role-playing game on a platform that has very few of them. There are two fully voice acted campaigns (plus a third that I think is a DLC?) with towns, sidequests, shops, and lots of things to do. There are experience and level up mechanics, but I haven't played far enough to get to use them yet. If this is the sort of thing you've been looking for on the Quest, buy it.

reviews.review_title: Big Miss: No Spectator Mode, Not Enough
reviews.review_description:
Love both this and the original Demeo, been playing for years, but there are two pretty obvious gaps:

1. No spectator mode
If a group is mid-run, at max characters, or just wants to stay 2-player, you’re locked out. There’s no way to join, hang out, or even just chat without interrupting the campaign. A simple spectator option would fix this and make the game way more social.

2. Not enough new campaigns
The existing ones are solid, but they get repetitive fast. This game needs a steady drip of new content. Even one new campaign a month would keep things fresh and give people a reason to keep coming back.
You’re already working within a D&D-style framework, so expanding campaigns should be a priority. Other games are doing this well and staying relevant because of it.

I recently bought this game and I'm in love, it's so worth it and it's addictive. You get three campaigns and over 5 one-shot dungeons that you can replay with seven heroes. 

You get to play online with friends DND style.

The details in this game are nothing short of amazing.

Thank you

This is such a great upgrade to the regular Demeo. If you like Demeo, or any turn-based games, you will love this game.  It is also a great way to scratch that DND itch.  It may not have the full depth of playing the pen and paper version, but it incorporates a ton of the faves and really gives you that playing with miniatures feel.  Highly recommend this game!

Edit. pretty much everything I suggested in multiple reviews got read and implemented, great job to the Dev team. I will leave the previous review below to show how much improvement has been made.

Really hope some of these changes, ESPECIALLY difficulty settings, makes it into original Demeo

‐---------------------------

From better line of sight, a progression system and a way to delay your turn, to a narrative campaign this one is superior to Demeo in every way except enemies no longer break morale and run which I miss. 

Where Demeo was annoyingly difficult in later adventures, this one gets a bit too easy, but it is so much more fun. They both need difficulty settings.

 Also where Demeo gave too few cards this gives too many. I am constantly dumping cards because half my hand are reusable.I don't even want to take abilities that give cards like disarm because it will be one seldom used boss only type card permanently sitting in my hand taking up cap space. 

All static and ability cards should not count against the card cap imo, it really makes some classes have little room. Summon pets need more I in their AI. Summoning five rats and watching first couple needlessly run in oil then track it all over the map, next one runs through that tiny patch of Fire it never needed to go near then finds a reason to run back into the oil igniting it all blowing up half your team & all the rats except one who uses his turn to go attack harmless eggs freeing Fire serpents you now have to fight, sigh..... yes that really happened. These should not attack inanimate objects like eggs and spikes ever. Would be nice if they also avoided needlessly running through Fire and onto spikes, rats at least you don't expect to be too smart but the wolf companion should do better. They are pretty much a waste to use right now in my opinion and often more of a deterrent that you wish was dead.

The board is too easy to accidentally move when not using the controllers, please make this action take both hands. Also, all party members can level up in SP but in MP only your hero can, please change that. SP is already easier, let henchmen level. 

 Change misses so hitting a friendly is more unlikely. Once in a while can be funny, multiple times every dungeon level by multiple party members not so much.

All in all a great game though. Really hope they're almost done releasing new adventures and that eventually they will give players the ability to create their own

reviews.review_title: D and D lite meets Hero's Quest
reviews.review_description: Card based D and D game that feels like I'm playing inside my childhood favorite board game Hero's Quest.  Well worth the gold.  Especially if you want to play some D and D with friends at a distance.

reviews.review_title: Love the game but how about choices
reviews.review_description:
Just like Demeo, Demeo D&D is a fantastic game but…

C’mon!  Where are the male people?  I *have* no choices to the characters being male or female. You must know that most people playing these games are male so sure, keep the female characters but ALLOW THE USER TO CHOOSE MALE OR FEMALE. 

It lessens the game to hear your character sound like you’ve been kicked in the n*ts. 

Male character choices, please!  And ya, it matters (like all the other games that let you choose).
I would have give 5 stars but after update the map gets blurry sometimes and then back to clarity and on on blurry , clarity ! Please help I like very much this game!

developer_response.body:
Hello, thank you for bringing this issue to our attention. We will look into this. If you want to share more details such as what headset you used and on what level this occurred, please reach out to us via our support form https://resolutiongames.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/requests/new
or on Discord: https://discord.com/invite/Fb2hVZsV4K

reviews.review_title: Very good, but can be very frustrating.
reviews.review_description:
If you like demeo, this is the same, but with faster card play. You get more cards so you can actually play a special card about every two-three regular 'attack' card.

I found frustrating that the dice are at times  landing even more frequent on 'miss' than the original demeo.

Like now, I just stopped playing the first frost keep. 3 ice giants, oooookaay. But getting a miss on almost 1/3 of every dice roll? Assassin death stab, miss, damaged reflected, assassin dead. Rejuvenation card from bard. Assassin stealth, backstab, double dice miss?!? Really? Every 1 on 3 dice rolls is a miss with every character. After trying to keep away swarms from blocking the exit this way for half hour, I just turned the game off. There is absolutely no way where 1/3 rolls come up as misses knowing how many and what faces the dice have.

Like I said, good game, yes, but... Dice rolls like that, no... Higher chance to win the lottery 10 weeks in a row.

Second day, second try... I got through... Somehow... Then the second floor had more of the same dice BS. Until after playing 15 minutes and moving a character, just out of nothing, I get 3 frost giants just appearing out of nothing in between my party. 

Dice rolls BS AND randomly appearing enemies?

Done with this...

developer_response.body:
Hi,
in the latest update we added a feature - Tymorra's Blessing - that gives you advantage on the next roll if you roll a miss, this should make the dice roll frustration  hit a bit less.  We use a twenty sided die with two misses, so one in ten rolls should statistically be a miss. 

With the last update we also added a feature for the One Shot dungeons where you can turn of the randomly respawning enemies if you want.

If you want you can join our Discord community to get updates from us developers but also talk to other people playing the game: http://discord.gg/Fb2hVZsV4K

Hope this helps!

reviews.review_title: The gameplay isn't as fun as Demeo
reviews.review_description: Although this game has more going on - voice acting, character upgrades, side quests, biomes, - the gameplay isn't nearly as fun as the gameplay in Demeo. The reasons being:                                                                                                    1. The game isn't very strategic. In Demeo you are constantly making hard decisions between revealing rooms, attacking, looting, etc. In this game you often will travel to one open area and just move from enemy to enemy attacking.                                                                                                                                                      2. You draw way to many cards. In Demeo you always have to really need a card when you play it, since gaining cards is so hard. But in this game, you are constantly getting cards for seemingly no reason, meaning you almost never need to do basic melee attacks.                                                                                               3. The story seems to be overly-complicated, especially for a game that is meant to be played in short sessions with friends.                                                                             4. In general, the game is just extremely easy, especially with 4 characters, and it doesn't have excitement like in Demeo.

reviews.review_title: Amazing game, don’t like pre-gen heroes
reviews.review_description:
I am having an amazing time playing Battlemarked. The only let down is the pre generated heroes. I hope there is a roadmap to getting customizable heroes. Where players can choose class and species. Also a bigger variety of different equipment drops beyond weapons.

Overall very fun and fast pace D&D experience.

Take the Demeo formula, add a pair of massive D&D-style campaigns to it with voice acting, RPG-style dialog trees, random D&D-style encounters, a boatload of locales, character customization and leveling, and wrap it into an even more polished package that works with VR and non-VR platforms, alike. Mixed reality is just the cherry on top, making this feel like a proper "D&D night" every time I play.
just get it

reviews.review_title: Demeo, dungeons and dragons battle mark
reviews.review_description: It has been a really great game very intriguing and exciting. When will there be more DLC’s campaigns looking forward to them￼
Fantastic game, hopefully bring out more DLC‘s and more games! ￼

Before I get into this, here is a disclaimer: ive never touched D&D in my life; always wanted to but never got to. Me and a buddy have been playing through the campaigns and having an absolute blast(he never played d&d either). Demeo was a good start but its literally a dungeon crawler; this game has campaigns as well as dungeons, the characters level and you can choose what happens when they level. Its difficulty compared to Demeo is lowered, for those who felt Demeo was too intense as well.

Play Battlemarked, you will not regret it! And its 1000 times more fun with a friend.
it's just like sitting playing D&D in my living room or basement from the 80's. Great narration, battles, spells, skills. So much more fun with others.

We enjoy this game, love the scenes! I think what needs to be fixed is, when coming into the game as multiplayer is the first player gets to start with 3 hero’s and the second person starts with 1 hero! Why does this happen? Also when getting skulls, and trust me, there are a lot of skulls , when you have so many enemies around you! we shouldn’t be hitting our own players!

Great game, fantastic with others.
has a lot of potentiel and looking forward to were it goes.
story is weak, but has some random events which can bothe help and hinder you. 
Its not demeo, its demeo like.
been have a lot of enjoyment with the game and think its worth asking price. 
I have it quest and pc 

dont buy it only for the story.
This is truly a top tier game, I absolutely love it, it just needs more content, which I'm sure will come.

I never played the first Demeo this was my first Demeo game. I feel like in the next update you guys should add a new character. I find the game absolutely incredible if you play mixed reality, I just wish I had a friend to play with the game is truly a work of art 
(￼￼￼But I wish it was on Xbox) 
I’d give this game a 9/10
I love the level design and storytelling overall great game

reviews.review_title: Stop giving 1 star for player overload.
reviews.review_description:
Great game, looks great, and plays great! I’ve also been a big fan of the musical score. 

The devs will fix issues for everyone whining about their multiplayer not working. It’s day one my fellow humans.. give them some slack.

Keep it up devs!! Been playing demeo and demeo battles since day one!

I gotta tell you...I like Demeo a lot. But I adore this game.

You already read those other reviews. You know you wanna buy it. So just do it. Just don't go spending rent money to do it...on second thought, might still be worth it.

Its that fun.

The Warlock are fun.
Having said that... The game is very difficult now, the AI is over the top. 
Play easy and no respawns if you want to have fun and casual mode 
Very hard and respawns is crazy hard.

Seriously delivered on gameplay, content, mexhanics…only complaint is no animation on the characters. I get that they want the feel of a table top… it this is VR…I’d rather have an immersive experience.  Bring these characters to life!!

Seriously good job!!

They took Demeo and cranked it up to 11! Demeo D&D Battlemarked makes a massive comeback with Quest Achievements, which was lacking in Battles. They've added more story elements, character customization, and ability checks - such as rolling a die to complete interactions or search your surroundings. We will be spending our game nights here!

Gameplay video and Affiliate Link on socials - M4cheteColletti

Got it on the first day. Play it everyday since that. Waiting for new Heroes an a new Kampagne.

One Question: Is there a crossbuy Option for the rift store, so i can play it as a pcvr titel? Didnt try it yet.

So I like the game so far...Just not super wow'd by it. It's basically Demeo with a few elements of D&D sprinkled here and there. Especially in character creation and development. You have a choice of just 6 character classes and the race of each class is limited to the one assigned to it. You do raise up in levels with each one giving you an ability point assigned to it. Unfortunately you're only given the choice of one of 3 abilities per point. As far as character stats? You're limited to a primary and secondary stat. Not 6 like in D&D. It definitely has super cool graphics with stunning colors but that's really the only part where it super shines to me. It is a nice advance from demeo but definitely only a micron of characteristics of D&D (I had hoped it was going to be more D&D and less Demeo) Overall I'm sorta mostly under-whelmed with it. It is fun to play but I don't see myself playing for hours like other games I enjoy playing. I'd give it 3.5 stars but the option for half stars isn't possible so giving it a 3 because it's closer to 3 star than 4 star. I expected a lot more for a $30 game app. Wait for it to go on sale. Hoping for future updates to character creation and development. If I see that I will revise my review. Until them it's just an "ok" from me.

reviews.review_title: I want to love this game but
reviews.review_description: I tried really hard to like this game. I loved the intro, all the fights, accepting side quests etc. but the second you step into your first dungeon, the game just becomes exponentially hard for no reason, and doesn't give you really any guidance on what you should be doing. It feels like you've been playing two totally different games when you get to the first dungeon. The first few fights give you a good idea of what to do, and are really easy. They're also very eye-catching and have interesting 3-D rendered environments, and very obviously tell you where to go or what to do. When I entered the first dungeon (a mushroom cave area), each floor has the same 3-D textures and blue lights on every level that become stale at the second level because of the variety that came before. I actually liked the exploration part of this, although again, this wasn't super explained to me as the only other dungeon before this is a tutorial where it explicitly tells you where to go. The tutorial prepares you to find a key that I could not find. Somehow I got it accidentally, and then the enemies infinitely spawn so you can barely move between elementally affected tiles and enemies everywhere. When you do get to the exit, if there are enemies nearby, sometimes they block the exit? even if all your characters are next to it. There is barely enough time to loot the chests (half the fun of an RPG for me), because as soon as you have cleared a room and turn away, enemies respawn everywhere, causing you to get overloaded and punished for exploration. The game pushes you to the exit as fast as possible, like it doesn't want you to enjoy it. There's also no need to loot chests. I was "max money" before I had finished the first floor and every enemy drops cards as loot so your hand is almost always too full to get any loot. Just to give you an example of how the game doesn't explain things very well - the dungeon suddenly became a lot easier when I realised you could level up your characters skills - something I guessed since I saw XP markers after every battle, but I genuinely don't recall being taught about in any tutorial or introduction. A tree skill system that looks very convoluted, is rather hidden and isn't easily explained just turns me off learning it at all. I'd like to give this another go as the graphics and music are incredible - the beginning of the game is legit the most fun I've had in VR so far! I guess it's just a skill issue for me? It would be nice for this to be more accessible though - perhaps with a difficulty level. Alas, for now it seems, my dungeon crawling will be no more as this game sadly just frustrates me. Perhaps I will give the multiplayer a go and see if my experience is improved, but for single players, I would avoid this one unless you're big on strategy games. It's not really an RPG or a board game. Certainly not for casual players.

developer_response.body:
Hi,
too bad to hear that you find the game to difficult. I know playing multiplayer is not for everyone, but I'd just like to say that we have a great community of players happy to help beginners navigate the dungeons.
We also have a very active Discord community where you can ask questions and keep up to date on improvements being made on the game (hint: we are addressing some of you concerns in future updates). You can join the Discord here: http://discord.gg/Fb2hVZsV4K

reviews.review_description: This games is so bad with how easy it is to miss click, place the wrong spell card, and make the wrong move by accident. Or when you try to melee an enemy and you some how didn’t directly aim at it and instead of doing an attack you just sit next to the enemy, in what world would I want to just chill next to an enemy and not attack? Not only this but the amount of times I’ve rolled critical fails and how absolutely minimal I’ve rolled criticals is absurd, if both or supposedly “infrequent” then I should be getting just as many criticals as critical fails. Things like this make the quality of life in this game so bad, there needs to be a way to solidify your choices before they are finalized because this is ridiculous when every move counts and you end up doing something completely different then you intended because the games UI and move management is so bad.

This game overall is great,but it has so much potential and I personally think that a custom mode should be added where you can create your own campaigns but there be no voice lines but you can host your own (will need a mic or text feature included,yes I know there is a mic feature but pls add a text feature).Make a menu on your wrist where you can place terrain and props.Also add a “undo” feature if a mistake is made.Also instead of free snap add a clip green circle where the terrain your holding is clipped on but easy to fix.make a animation feature with an animation the creator can edit and use in the campaign plus when an achievement is made.Please add these into categories that are on the wrist menu.that’s all my ideas for now pls leave a 👍so the devs can see this. Thanks -ghost

The game itself is fun.
Can't be installed on Meta Horizon Link PC to play through PC

If you plan on a demeo style dnd, expect neither. This took some of the least enjoyable aspects of both and snuk em in as core mechanics, i swear to - do not use a rogue or tank like you do in demeo, quickest way to full nuke your group. Stealth opening a door doesn't mean like in demeo that your team isn't going to be ambushed by poor fast enemies that cant open doors so you will be very rightly in sadly the party's eyes be kick for effectively killing them all because stealth scouting isn't a strategic or tactical asset, it's a massive liability. Meanwhile your tank is on 2 hp because damage is required for effectively every enemy being a troll or ogre in hp that's named for any reason and smaller enemies being all elite archers arcanists or greater spiders, so you end up with no healers or the infamous broken cleric for any form of real tanking while you're left with what we all know to be the just fine line tuned tank from demeo that's instead fighting bosses every room with no healer while the other classes get massive damage ups on the skill tree of 9 max skill additions in the what id basically describe as MTG or slay the spire cards as a skill system, and due to it being expensive be prepared to play solo for a long time or be kicked toxically from every lobby because they are allowed to see your unleveled character, this game will not be fun for random que casuals expecting to play with other vr peeps. Also don't worry, for the tactically sound you'll suffer too because you now have to bounce from session to session so you can get enough fake xp to be allowed to not be kicked regardless of your tactical or strategic prowess.

So this is a warning to those wanting anything related to the title, your eyes decieve you, this isn't either game but more of what they attempted to make as a restrictive even by demeo or dnd standards game that falls short of both on the board but tries to makeup in visual/audio/music/and choice mechanic details that only benefit... Well really just wizards which isn't surprising as they're normally the worst of the few spellcasters in dnd due to spell choice.

If you expected the mechanics of both games to be flipped on its' head though, by all means, mediocre game that executes the storytelling visuals that dnd always lacked. While being (for those who know this game) basically 'dnd/medieval doorkickers.' Great concept of 1 room only combat, but you hurt your scouts and tanks and healers so much that a literal lure would do a better job than 3/6 of your class choices and live longer too. 

Not by any stretch a bad game but not by any stretch dnd/demeo either and i have a long history of playing both in most versions. If you want what you expected this game to be for the dnd peeps, go snag 'Neverwinter:Nights' its this but fleshed out for your campaign needs and has more campaigns multiplayer than this game probably ever will but that remains to be seen as both end and demeo are known for adding more but they fell short for now and got some catchup to do to fix core mechanic problems (namely stealth scouting and 4 turn forced cooldown that promotes leaving your allies to die otherwise you die with them, best of luck main dps roles) and lack of miriads of options from both games issue because demeo had the great formula for combat here and so does dnd but they both threw away ambush and stealth systems that are thoroughly tested for a half baked anti stealth approach that makes your team and yourself hate you more than any dnd rogue, and that's saying something.

Honestly good game that has much needed polish that even other users know is missing for now, keep an eye out on if they fix these issues publically cause the game will be better for it.
Different gameplay in some mechanics. Overall still fun. I wish theybkept the original Bard though. I guess I'd grown attached.

reviews.review_title: Broken - bug report button broke too
reviews.review_description:
Great start.... (new update at bottom after 2 hours - then after 4 hours) DO NOT BUY.

Apparently our room codes are INVALID and OFFLINE.

1 minute the friends list is blank, cant search anyone or invite via meta invites, nedt minute we Apparently have 99 notifications to which we open and it is also blank.

We press REPORT BUG which hilariously doesnt do anything.

Imagine if a game released, was tested, and just worked after paying for it.

We will wait an hour and if still broke then 4 refunds will be made.

Good job team.

(Update reply to Devs)

I can assure you the Report Button DIDNT work, we pressed it multiple times (3 people) if there was some odd delay which it seems you are saying then that also means it doesnt work... didnt know I had to sit and wait for the Report button to randomly decide to work.

Atleast you apologised in the response... oh wait...

(Update after 2 hours)

So after Mr Dev assured me it seems I wasnt pressing the bug Report button properly, that STILL does nothing. 

The game STILL is unplayable online, all though when you click invite friend on the character select screen you get a nice red line of text that looks like jumbled up letters across the screen, is that also working as intended?? 

What a shambles.

Where is the 0 stars option?

(4 Hours later)

And it still doesnt work, funny how the original reply was some hiccups but sorted or along those lines, yet now there is a full news page explaining the issue and to go on discord (can we play it there??)

If the game is now single player only can we have 50% of the cost refunded?

Also you may want to amend the information on the store page becuase this line is currently a great joke

 - "EPIC QUESTS. INSTANT ADVENTURES"

Epic quests - instant multiplayer bugs.

developer_response.body:
If you want you can join our discord here and get help from our community (and us) more directly: http://discord.gg/Fb2hVZsV4K

_______________________________
Update: The game should be more stable now. We have also patched issues that many players has been facing with both network and gameplay. Please consider updating your review if it has solved the issues you've been experiencing. We have started looking into changing the ways hireling works in singleplayer so stay tuned for that update in the near future.

reviews.review_description:
Within an hour of release there were several 1-3 star reviews sitting bugs etc. Don’t listen to them, as a first release, it’s extremely stable and bug free.

There are some wrinkles of course. I read one review that said they couldn’t exit. I had the same problem until I realized there was a popup dialog box outside my field of view. Once I clicked OK, I was able to exit.

I’m sure the wrinkles will get smoothed out. It’s a really fantastic new version of Demeo that is everything the original game should have been.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the review!

There are a few issues we're not happy to have in the game, and the team takes great pride in their work so it'll be something that we address—even if it seems minor, we want to resolve them regardless!

reviews.review_description: If you're looking for a fun strategy game tabletop with good graphics to play single player or up to 3 other friends this and all other Demeo games are IT...so happy my friends and myself bought this..looking forward to new maps and characters 😝

reviews.review_title: Lots of fun for me and my friends
reviews.review_description:
Absolutely love multiplayer with my friends online. Great times!
Single-player is great too.

I really like this game! But to be very honest, I get more anxious to play Demeo becuase of the animations of the character. When I played on VR I was looking forward to seeing it would be incorporated, in which I didnt see in the demo. So although I gave it a 5 star, it is very deserving, but I really wish there was more to animations. That part of gaming is what keeps me playing Demeo, its like a show! Not a fan of stiff statues with the hop and jump kicks, it just eh and less exciting. Just my opinion, but I really would like to see that part excel. Thank you!

Please for the love .. make purchasable expansion campaigns a la beat saber and just keep the new content flowing!.. $10 bucks for a new campaign.. I'll pay it all day..

This game is just fantastic.. everything that vr promises.. immersion multi-player ability to set your own viewpoint..

If you are looking at this review and considering buying.. just do it you won't be disappointed 

To the dev team.. expansion packs as paid add on content..  happy to fund you guys doing more work

Please give me the opportunity..

Anyone giving this game 1 star for having trouble with multiplayer in its first few hours of release is out of their minds.

I was a day late to the party, so granted I didn't have that frustration, but to rate it 1 star because you have 5 minutes of attention span and patience is ridiculous.  This game is everything I always wanted Demeo to be.  Stories, quests, side quests, dialogue choices, a world map not unlike the og Dark Alliance games, skill checks, and an AWESOME talent system. You can customize your character's colors, name, and weapons (this part could be expanded on, but it's a big leap forward).  All of this new stuff on top of the super successful combat system Demeo has (which sort of plays like 4E D&D or the D&D board game systems).  I played much of today with friends, connecting several times because battery and literally my battery life was the only frustration.  Room codes work perfectly, most of my friends were on steam so crossplay is flawless.  Honestly 11/10, really really well-developed evolution of Demeo.  Well done Devs, and thank you!
enjoying it alot wish the charters had more animations tho
I really enjoy how the game is designed to allow you to play solo or in a group

Amazing game and concept. I bought it an hour ago and I want to love it, but I’m hitting a few issues:

I can’t host a game: it keeps giving me a network error, but my connection is perfectly fine.

I also can’t join public games. Any idea why this happens?

And lastly, I usually play Demeo on my recliner and rely on tilt mode. Tilt is enabled in Options /Graphics, but nothing tilts in-game.

I’ve got about 3 hours left before I can request a refund. If I don’t hear anything from a dev soon, I’ll probably have to go that route, which would be a shame because the game looks fantastic.

developer_response.body:
Hi,

We're sorry that you had a bad experience with our game. Our servers reached capacity quite quickly, and the team had to do some troubleshooting to get them stable again. The network errors, and difficulty joining public games, should be resolved now, and we are making more improvements to the servers.

In order to use the tilt function in Battlemarked, hold down the right grip button and use the thumbstick to tilt. For hand-tracking: tilt the board bringing the thumb of your right hand across your palm, and untilt by doing the same action with the other hand.

Please join our discord for more updates and get help from our community (and us): http://discord.gg/Fb2hVZsV4K

Thanks!

_______________________________
Update: The game should be more stable now. We have also patched issues that many players has been facing with both network and gameplay. Please consider updating your review if it has solved the issues you've been experiencing. We have started looking into changing the ways hireling works in singleplayer so stay tuned for that update in the near future.

reviews.review_title: What I'd hoped Demeo would be!
reviews.review_description:
I've had Demeo for years and while I really wanted to love it, it's just too ridiculously difficult for me to enjoy.

Adding Dungeons & Dragons into the mix is just what Demeo needed to work for me. So far I've played around an hour of the first solo campaign. I was lucky to last 15 minutes in Demeo before it was Game Over.

I love the traditional Main Quest + Side Quests RPG format.

The graphics are beautiful, (as you'd expect from the Demeo team's talented artists), but the graphics engine seems to perform better. Even on my Quest 3 the original Demeo struggles at times. This runs well. Designing the characters as D&D miniatures that hop about the board is a nice touch!

Technically Demeo x Dungeons & Dragons feels more polished than the original. My only technical complaint is in regards to the way the board changes scale during transitions. I get the board set up just the way I want, then after a transition it's blown up so large that it's sticking through the walls and sitting on the floor and I have to shrink it back down again, (I play in MR mode). I tried changing the camera mode to see if that would fix it. (I have no idea what the camera setting does, it seems to have no effect on anything.)

This isn't a technical complaint, just something about the game design that I really don't like. In VR mode I would much prefer the VR environment act like a real environment and stay in a realistic scale, and manipulating the board would just change the board. Spinning around and changing scale like Alice in Wonderland is uncomfortable and immersion breaking. It ruins the VR mode for me. An option to switch this on and off would be very much appreciated.

If you're like me, (and many others judging by some of Demeo's negative reviews), and you found Demeo frustratingly difficult you may want to give this a go.
For anyone who grew up playing D&D with minis, this is like mainlining nostalgia.

reviews.review_title: Fun game needs a lot of improvements
reviews.review_description:
When the game first released they had a decent cadence to bug fixes and updates to the game but it's been like a month and a half now with no new patches or updates and there are numerous bugs and performance issues that have not been addressed. 

Unfortunately it also seems like the player base has dwindled significantly because nowadays there's maybe one or two public one-shots open when searching for a game.

People also take ages to use their turn and decide what their going to do on their action. Even though it should be common sense to be thinking about what you're going to do when it's not your turn, basically nobody does that. This draws the games out way longer than they need to be.

Overall i do still recommend it but they really need to start putting out more updates if they want this game to not die.

Demeo Battlemarked completely blew past my expectations. It feels like classic Demeo, but expanded into a full Dungeons & Dragons–style campaign, and it absolutely nails the atmosphere. The graphics are stunning, the narration adds a ton of immersion, and the quests are genuinely interesting. Playing with friends has been a blast, and honestly, buying multiple copies was worth every cent.
I’m really excited to see what expansions or new campaigns get released, and I’m really hoping the devs add a Dungeon Master–style tool for creating custom campaigns or one-shot missions. Right now, this is easily one of the top games on Quest 3 for me—standing right alongside Dungeons of Eternity.

The best D&Dish videogame I've played - extremely fun with friends too, I just wish the shop hub world from demeo was included in this 

This is also a graphical beauty!

I'm in disbelief about how the core mechanics of this game are so bad compared to Demeo.  If I didn't know any better and you told me this game was made before Demeo I would have believed you.  This team spent a long time in the dark rewriting Demeo and I thought it was in preparation for this game but now it's unclear what that was about as it's not reflected in this game and they haven't delivered any new Demeo content.  Like Demeo, I suspect the devs will continue to spend hours on features that aren't value add.

developer_response.body:
Hi! I'm sorry to hear that you feel that way about the game. We tried our best to create something that built upon the system from Demeo, while also adding new features such as a deeper narrative, character progression, weapon drops and skill checks. That said, we will continue to work on the game and keep following valuable player feedback to improve it. 

I hope that over time, you give us another shot and find the game is more enjoyable to you. If you'd like to talk more with the community or the team, please consider joining our Discord. Here is a link: http://discord.gg/Fb2hVZsV4K

Thank you.

reviews.review_description:
This game has an identity problem of what it wants to be, card game, or turn based RPG. I honestly don’t see how it has such a high review score. Got further into the game and the mobs were non-stop with unlimited explosive poison artillery from ungodly ranges. I uninstalled the game last night and slept on it before making this review. 

The last thing I will mention is the character creation system is mid at best. Essentially you’re stuck with 6 characters with locked in race and class where you can only change the name. Even character progression is highly limited.

The only thing that makes this even remotely close to DnD is the story is set in Forgotten Realms. 

The one nice thing I will say is it is visually really cool. If they just took DnD beyond and merged it with this table top design it would be amazing. 

Don’t have to trust my opinion but it takes A LOT for me to actually write a bad review for game. Take that for what’s it’s worth.
my wife and i have been playing this and it's a blast. It's become one of our date night games.
An easy 5 stars, thank you for this delightful experience.
Five been waiting on agame like this foot a long time
This game is amazing.  I cannot wait to see the additional Campaigns that they come out with.  Really looking forward to it
Better than regular Demeo...almost!

reviews.review_title: Great but a few launch issues
reviews.review_description:
Love original Demeo, love this too. Fantastic aesthetic and worlds, really worth the wait. Will be my favourite game in vr for plenty of time to come I am sure.

A frustrating issue I must mention though is the card discard UI element is much more sensitive and position changed too. Playing through the first campaign I accidentally discarded cards dozens and dozens of times whilst trying to play them or simply trying to check ranges. Never happened once in original Demeo. I realise it’s only just launched to the vr audience so plenty of things like this will crop up and be addressed in time.

Still amazing despite the teething issues. Look forward to seeing it when it’s had the final polishing touches added.
Awesome Game full of action

I only started playing Demeo a week ago then I saw this land in the store . 
What a great game I have never played any sort of D&D style of game before I must say I am totally sucked into it .
It's really easy for a complete novice like myself  to get the hang of its well worth the purchase.

Gameplay is easy once you get to know your cards though it can get difficult when your deep in battle.
The graphics are stunning the characters look really nice and detailed up close .
The board is fantastic and the way you can move the table is really nice.
This is how modern tabletop gaming should be! I hope they do some more campaigns.

Battlemarked delivers exactly the experience I hoped for: it feels like a natural evolution of Demeo. In many ways, it plays like the true successor to Demeo — familiar mechanics, but elevated with smart improvements that make the whole experience more dynamic and satisfying.

The atmosphere, the flow of combat, and the tactical depth all mirror the best parts of Demeo, yet Battlemarked builds on them with more variation, cleaner systems, and a stronger sense of progression. It’s clear the developers studied what made Demeo great and then polished everything from the UI to abilities, mission structure, and pacing.

There are a few small bugs, but nothing that breaks the game. More importantly, they feel like issues that will be fixed soon rather than signs of deeper problems.

If you love Demeo, Battlemarked feels like coming home — just to a house that has been renovated in all the right ways. Strongly recommended.

reviews.review_title: So lacking,  buggy and not finished
reviews.review_description: I dont this is a finshed game. This game has so many things going for it if they would just finish it. But the game seems to be released unfinished.  It is buggy and quirky with the controllers pointer shifting every 30 seconds of not pushing a button, which returns to point after pushing a button. Has a create character button,  but you can't create any character, only change the name of the 6 pc created characters or varying shade of same clothes. There is no mixed reality like demo where you can see around you with the board in the room rather than a vr room. If this is in the original demeo game it wouldnt be too hard to have this in the game.

Now that the vanishing cards bug is fixed I have updated my review to five stars. If you liked the original demeo but want something with more of a story and more varied environments then you can't go wrong.

I love this game just like the original. it truly reminds me of plain dunges and dragons with my friends the only difference is know that it's in vr. i gave it four stalls only because the game randomly crashes on my meta quest three. it's annoying to be in the middle of a game just for it to just close out and you have to start back. regardless of that you can have countless hours of fun with this game.

reviews.review_title: UPDATE Forgot to turn the servers on!!!!
reviews.review_description:
Almost 4 hours and online servers still don't work.

All off the following issues still happening but still get a disconnected from server message.

At least Devs added a "News" update to announce their shoddy servers, lets all play  DnD, a multiplayer social game on our own.

£24 for half a game.



A "Multiplayer" game, you can only play solo. None of the online features work (including the "Report Bug" button).

Can't search for friends, shows 99 notifications until you click and there's none. Still shows 99 when you go back.

developer_response.body:
We've had some server issues on and off, but most of them should be fixed. Sorry about that!

If you want you can join our discord here and get help from our community (and us): http://discord.gg/Fb2hVZsV4K

_______________________________
Update: The game should be more stable now. We have also patched issues that many players has been facing with both Network and gameplay. Please consider updating your review if it has solved the issues you've been experiencing. We have started looking into changing the ways hireling works in singleplayer so stay tuned for that update in the near future.

reviews.review_description: i don't know what's going on but this keeps happening constantly...my cards will just disappear when i try and play them and nothing happens. other than that the game looks amazing and has a lot of depth to it that i wish demeo battles had. i haven't played online yet but judging by what i've played so far this might be a five star game i will change my review once they fix this bug.

developer_response.body:
Hi!

We're happy to see you're enjoying the game, and are currently investigating the card disappearing issue. Thank you so much for taking the time to review and send us this report.

For more updates you can join our discord here and interact more with the community (and us): http://discord.gg/Fb2hVZsV4K

reviews.review_description:
This game is an extremely beautiful masterpiece. It plays easy enough (especially for demeo players who are used to the game mechanism) and it has two great campaigns. The figurines are wonderful, there is humor in the storylines, and there are sidequests to do. You can play it singleplayer or multiplayer just like demeo. 

Is this DND? No it's not. It's more demeo with a storyline. And you fight with magical cards, just like in demeo (totally not like in DND). But that's ok, it's different, it has some similar ideas, and it's extremely good in it's own right. You can't unfortunately create your own character, you just choose one of the characters and you play with that one. Roleplay is very limited like any computer roleplaying game. The storylines are quite thin, I could feel the outcome from the beginning but there's only one path to follow, you can't really do anything else besides doing all the combats. There's no way to avoid them or to do a creative solution. 

I still see this more like a heroquest boardgames than a DND roleplaying evening. Which is ok, as heroquest is a magnificent game, and this goes way beyond heroquest. 

Only downside to me, is the replayability. Compared to demeo and demeo battles, I don't feel the urge to revisit the same campaign. While the chatter was nice to follow the first time, if you play it again it becomes a little boring. You know what is going on. Also, compared to demeo, I prefered to play this singleplayer instead of multiplayer. 

Verdict : this game is amazing, I enjoyed it a lot. I do prefer demeo and demeo battles though, for the replayability. I would have given this 5/5 if you could create your own hero, gor example an old human wizard like gandalf, a half-orc barbarian, or a druid... Who knows, one day... :)

Just like the title says, this game has a lot of promise,  quick enough to get through the tutorial but trying to get a game online is a nightmare,  developers are aware of this though and I look forward to playing some more, then I can change my review to what will probably be a well deserved 4/5 stars.

developer_response.body:
HI! You are correct that we are aware of the server issues. Sorry about that. It should be more stable now, and the team is looking into more improvements as we speak.
Thanks for your patience! You can join our discord here to get both updates from us and help from our community: http://discord.gg/Fb2hVZsV4K

reviews.review_title: Fun, a great follow up to the original
reviews.review_description: Really enjoying it so far.  just as good as the original.
For a casual like me who wants to get into DND but doesn't want to be overwhelmed. This feels like the perfect introduction.

Beautiful graphics on the Quest 3, a perfect use of Demeo to explore the world and characters of Dungeons and Dragons.  Perfect right out of the gates, can't wait to see what the future holds.  

PS - now do a Warhammer x Demeo game!

reviews.review_title: Great idea, but game seems untested
reviews.review_description:
The tutorial is broken, can't proceed to exit.

Cards are tiny, almost unreadable.

"Magic wand" pointer from both hands makes using your motion controllers as hands difficult.

Multiplayer doesn't work as it is supposed to.

I love the 1st Demeo game, it's highly polished compared to this.

This was on my wishlist for several months, and I'm highly disappointed.

developer_response.body:
Hi! Sorry to hear you've been having issues with the game. 

We've been going through all player feedback and issues and will be addressing them as soon as we can. 

As you have probably seen, our servers have been under high load and we've been doing our best to stabilize them. We hope to improve them further so you can give Multiplayer another shot!

We will keep working on improving the game, and hope we can bring you the experience you were hoping for over time. 

Thanks!

reviews.review_description: This game perfectly takes the mechanics of demeo and adds in stellar voice acting, two great campaigns and character upgrades. So much fun!

reviews.review_title: Easy to tell the special needs reviews
reviews.review_description: Seriously you gotta be some kinda stupid to give a game a 1 star review minutes after it being released some of these people leaving the one star reviews are exactly why you can't trust people saying a game isn't good if you haven't tried it for yourself, it's a great game for a good price as long as they keep releasing campaigns it will be a must have, not many sit down VR games that compare to it and I was surprised it was even cheaper than their previous games, that was definitely a smart move at a time when everything is getting more expensive I honestly thought it would be a 50$ game...

So it IS amazing, well balanced, looks great, cool stories, great gameplay and just generally awesome to play with friends 

BUT
1 I think the hireling thing is silly, if we're doing a two man campaign let us have two of our characters each


2  the host/person who has the character that goes first ends up making a lot of the ability checks/decisions I think it would be cool to be able to defer to a friend who's character is better suited

3 one or two level ups seem KINDA underwhelming, like on sorcerer going from "magic dart" which is so cool and useful to "8% chance to reflect attacks but only if they're ranged spells" feels a little bit disappointing 

Now despite the kart three paragraphs it is genuinely awesome and I cannot recommend enough

What a great adaption of the DnD Universe. Keep up the good work! My suggestion: how about a Warhammer 40k adaption? Possibly in the style of Kill Team? That would really fit your system and mechanics like a glove.

Monka schlonka ist der Multiplayer geil! Weitere Charaktere und lvl Möglichkeiten würden das Spiel noch geiler machen und ein System bei dem man weitere Erungenschaften/freischaltungen erreichen kann. Ansonsten ist das Spiel relativ schnell durchgespielt. Ich hoffe es wird stetig weiter entwickelt und die Decks bringen bald ein paar coole Erweiterungen raus. Von mir aus auch kostenpflichtig. Geiles Game!

Ran through the first campaign with a friend and we both absolutely loved it. We got through the campaign in maybe 12 hours over three or four long sessions (because we couldn’t stop playing). 

Couple of niggly bits:

1) with just the two of us we can only use one of our personal heroes, the other hero that we each control seems to be outside of our personal hero collections. Would be good to each bring two of our own heroes along for the campaign.

2) In a hand full of instances a character would be outside of a bosses red highlighted AOE attacks but still take damage.

3) in the latest session on 10/01/2025 the rogue being in stealth did nothing to stop any and every enemy from going after them, making stealth a bit redundant.

Still, we had an absolute blast, can’t wait to get stuck into the 2nd campaign and really hope we see more and more campaigns and possibly a could have new heroes down the line!

I tried Demeo and I liked it and the same day I saw this game ,it’s really really good ,better then Demeo,u can level u heroes and its more cards.I am 42 and its really hard for me to play something interesting and now I can say I really really liked this game.. till now what I had tried-it’s the best game in store
